name: youtube-api-skill
tagline: ClawHub (142★): YouTube Data API integration with managed OAuth. Search videos, manage playlists, access channel data, and interact with co
verdict: Solid and safe: clean security, tidy hygiene, and a genuinely thorough API reference. The catch is write actions (create playlist, subscribe, comment) never check if the thing already exists first, so a retry after a hiccup can silently duplicate it. The 450-line inline catalog also bloats every load.

highlights.summary: Write operations (Create Playlist, Subscribe to Channel, Add Comment, Add Video to Playlist) have no mention of checking for an existing equivalent resource before creating; re-run
~450 lines of per-resource API catalog (Search/Videos/Channels/Playlists/PlaylistItems/Subscriptions/Comments, each with request+response examples) sit inline in SKILL.md with no s
Global install of the first-party CLI for the wrapped service from the standard npm registry, but unpinned (no version); same pattern in the Homebrew install line. Known publisher/
